design_summary: HIR (High-Level IR) with AST→HIR lowering, annotation validation, and basic type validation. The HIR mirrors the AST structure but adds unique node IDs, validates annotation placement, and checks that type references refer to known primitives or user-defined structs. This is Phase 1 — no full type checking, no name resolution beyond basic validation, no control-flow analysis.

files_to_create.purpose: HIR node type definitions — the core IR data structures

detailed_spec.overview: Defines the HIR node types that mirror the AST but with unique IDs on every node, validated annotations, and validated type references. Uses newtype ID wrappers for type-safe cross-references.

types.name: NodeId
types.doc: Unique identifier for every HIR node. Monotonically increasing, assigned during lowering.

types.name: FuncId
types.doc: Index of a function within HirModule.functions

types.name: ExprId
types.doc: Index into the expression arena within a function body

types.name: StmtId
types.doc: Index into the statement arena within a function body

types.name: NodeIdGen
types.doc: Monotonic ID generator for HIR lowering. Methods: new() -> Self, next(&mut self) -> NodeId

types.name: HirModule
types.doc: Top-level HIR module, the result of lowering an ast::Module.

types.name: HirFunction
types.doc: A function with validated annotations. @pure, @const, @inline, @complexity, @intent, @vectorizable, @parallel, @strategy, @constraint are valid on functions. @layout and @align are rejected on functions.

types.name: HirParam
types.doc: Function parameter with validated annotations. @layout and @align are valid on params. @pure is rejected on params.

types.name: HirStruct
types.doc: Struct definition with validated annotations.

types.name: HirStructField
types.doc: Struct field with validated annotations. @layout and @align are valid on fields.

types.name: HirTypeAlias
types.doc: Type alias: type Name = SomeType;

types.name: HirImport
types.doc: Import declaration.

types.name: HirBlock
types.doc: Block of statements. Blocks inside function bodies can carry @strategy annotations.

types.name: HirStmt
types.doc: Statement with unique ID and span.

types.name: HirExpr
types.doc: Expression with unique ID and span. Span is inherited from the AST — for AST nodes that carry spans directly, we propagate them; for nodes without explicit spans, we compute them from child spans.

variants.name: BinaryOp
variants.note: BinOp is re-exported from ast — identical enum, no desugaring needed at this level

types.name: HirType
types.doc: Validated type reference. During lowering, Named types are checked against the set of known primitives and user-defined struct names. Unknown types produce a LowerError but lowering continues (error recovery).

variants.name: Primitive
variants.doc: Resolved primitive type (i32, f64, bool, etc.)

variants.name: UserDefined
variants.doc: Resolved reference to a user-defined struct or type alias

variants.name: Unknown
variants.doc: Placeholder for types that failed validation — allows lowering to continue despite errors

types.name: PrimitiveType
types.doc: All primitive types from the AXIOM spec. Resolved from string names during lowering.

types.name: HirDimExpr
types.doc: Tensor dimension expression, mirrors ast::DimExpr.

types.name: HirAnnotation
types.doc: Annotation that has passed target validation.

types.name: HirAnnotationKind
types.note: InlineHint, LayoutKind, AnnotationValue, StrategyBlock, TransferBlock, OptLogEntry are re-exported from axiom_parser::ast. No need to duplicate these simple enums.

files_to_create.purpose: AST→HIR lowering with annotation validation and type validation

detailed_spec.overview: Walks the AST, produces HIR nodes, validates annotation placement and type references. Collects all errors rather than stopping at the first one. Returns Ok(HirModule) on success, Err(Vec<LowerError>) on failure.

public_api.doc: Lower an AST module to HIR. Validates annotation targets and type references. Returns all collected errors on failure.

fields.known_types: HashSet<String> — set of valid type names (primitives + user-defined structs + type aliases)

methods: fn new() -> Self — initializes with all primitive type names in known_types
fn collect_user_defined_types(&mut self, module: &ast::Module) — first pass: register all struct names and type aliases
fn lower_expr(&mut self, expr: &ast::Expr, span: Span) -> HirExpr — span passed down since ast::Expr does not carry its own span
fn lower_type(&mut self, ty: &ast::TypeExpr, span: Span) -> HirType — validates named types against known_types
fn validate_annotations(&mut self, annotations: &[HirAnnotation], target: AnnotationTarget, target_span: Span) — checks each annotation is valid for its target
fn resolve_primitive_type(name: &str) -> Option<PrimitiveType> — maps string to PrimitiveType enum

annotation_validation_rules.overview: Each annotation is only valid on certain targets. Invalid placement produces a LowerError::InvalidAnnotationTarget.

rules.note: Custom annotations allowed everywhere — they are for extensibility

type_validation_rules.overview: Named types must refer to a known primitive or a user-defined struct/type alias. Unknown types produce LowerError::UnknownType but lowering continues using HirType::Unknown.
algorithm: 1. First pass: scan all items to collect struct names and type alias names into known_types
2. Second pass: lower all items, resolving TypeExpr::Named against known_types
3. For Named types: if in primitives → HirType::Primitive, if in user_defined → HirType::UserDefined, else → HirType::Unknown + emit LowerError::UnknownType

lowering_details.expr_span_handling: The AST Expr enum does not carry spans directly. When lowering a Spanned<Stmt>, the stmt's span can serve as the expression span for top-level expressions in that statement. For nested expressions (BinaryOp children, Call args, etc.), we must synthesize a Span::DUMMY (0..0) since the AST doesn't track them. This is acceptable for Phase 1; Phase 2 can add span tracking to AST expressions.
lowering_details.desugaring: Phase 1 performs no desugaring — the HIR is structurally 1:1 with the AST. Future phases may desugar for/while into lower-level loop constructs.
lowering_details.two_pass_approach: The lowerer must do two passes: (1) collect all user-defined type names, (2) lower everything. This ensures forward references work (a function can reference a struct defined later in the file).

detailed_spec.overview: Defines LowerError enum with miette diagnostics for annotation validation failures, unknown type references, and duplicate definitions.

variants.name: InvalidAnnotationTarget

fields.target: String — what it was placed on (e.g., "let binding")
fields.valid_targets: String — comma-separated list of valid targets

variants.message: @{annotation} is not valid on {target}; valid on: {valid_targets}

variants.name: UnknownType
variants.help: known primitive types: i8, i16, i32, i64, i128, u8, u16, u32, u64, u128, f16, bf16, f32, f64, bool

variants.name: DuplicateModuleAnnotation
variants.message: multiple @module annotations found; only one is allowed

types.name: AnnotationTarget
types.doc: Identifies what kind of item an annotation is attached to, for validation purposes.

detailed_spec.overview: Implements Display for HirModule and all HIR node types. The output looks like AXIOM source code but with HIR-level detail: node IDs in comments, resolved types, validated annotations. Used by the CLI driver for --emit=hir.

public_api.doc: Convenience function that calls Display and returns the string.

output_format.description: The pretty-printer outputs AXIOM-like syntax annotated with node IDs. Indentation is 4 spaces. Annotations are printed on their own lines before the item they annotate.
example_output_for_fibonacci: for i: i32 in range(2, n + 1) {  // [node:10]

formatting_rules: Module-level annotations printed first, each on their own line
Annotations on items printed directly before the item, indented to match
Node IDs shown as // [node:N] comments at end of line for functions, annotations, and statements
Types printed in AXIOM syntax: i32, f64, tensor[f32, M, N], etc.
PrimitiveType variants map back to lowercase strings: I32 → 'i32', F64 → 'f64', Bf16 → 'bf16'
BinOp Display: Add→'+', Sub→'-', Mul→'*', Div→'/', Mod→'%', Eq→'==', NotEq→'!=', Lt→'<', Gt→'>', LtEq→'<=', GtEq→'>=', And→'and', Or→'or', etc.
Blocks: opening brace on same line, closing brace on own line at parent indent

new_content_spec.overview: Replace the stub with module declarations and re-exports
content: //! axiom-hir — High-level IR preserving semantic annotations.
//! The HIR is produced by lowering the AST. It validates annotation placement,
//! checks type references against known primitives and user-defined types,
//! and assigns unique node IDs for later compiler phases to reference.

changes.description: Add axiom-parser and axiom-lexer as dependencies (needed for ast types and Span)
Wire up --emit=hir to use axiom_hir::lower and display
changes.location: Replace the Some(level) catch-all arm for 'hir' with actual HIR lowering
new_logic: return Err(miette::miette!("parsing failed with {} error(s)", result.errors.len()));
return Err(miette::miette!("HIR lowering failed with {} error(s)", errors.len()));

architecture_decisions.rationale: Phase 1 simplicity. The HIR uses owned Box<HirExpr> rather than arena indices. Arena allocation can be added in Phase 2 if lowering performance matters. For fibonacci.axm with ~20 nodes, tree-based is simpler and fast enough.
architecture_decisions.alternative_considered: Arena-based with ExprId indices into Vec<HirExpr>
architecture_decisions.why_not: Adds complexity (index management, borrow checker dance) without Phase 1 benefit

architecture_decisions.decision: Re-export AST enums (BinOp, UnaryOp, etc.) rather than duplicating
architecture_decisions.rationale: BinOp, UnaryOp, InlineHint, LayoutKind, AnnotationValue, StrategyBlock, StrategyValue, TransferBlock, OptLogEntry are identical between AST and HIR. No desugaring changes them. Re-exporting avoids duplication and ensures consistency.

AXIOM allows forward references — a function can use a struct type defined later in the file. The first pass scans all items to build the set of known type names. The second pass does the actual lowering with complete type knowledge.

When a type name is not recognized, we emit an error but continue lowering using HirType::Unknown. This allows us to collect all errors in a single pass rather than stopping at the first unknown type.

architecture_decisions.decision: Span::DUMMY for expressions without source spans
architecture_decisions.rationale: The AST Expr enum does not carry spans. The Spanned<Stmt> wrapper provides a span for the whole statement, but sub-expressions lack precise spans. For Phase 1, we use a dummy span (0..0) for expressions. Phase 2 should add Span to ast::Expr.
FuncId, ExprId, StmtId newtypes are defined for future arena use but Phase 1 only uses NodeId directly on each struct. The arena IDs are reserved for Phase 2.

span_handling.strategy: Use axiom_lexer::Span throughout HIR. For nodes that have AST spans (via Spanned<T>), propagate the span. For ast::Expr (which has no span), use Span::new(0, 0) as a sentinel. The display.rs module does not depend on span accuracy for correct output.
span_handling.span_dummy_constant: Define pub const SPAN_DUMMY: Span = Span { start: 0, end: 0 }; in hir.rs

technical_spec.performance_notes: The lowering pass is O(n) in the number of AST nodes. For the benchmark criterion BC-1.3.1 (fibonacci.axm under 5ms), this is trivially satisfied — fibonacci.axm has ~25 AST nodes. No special optimization needed.

acceptance_tests.overview: Tests are organized by acceptance criteria ID from M1.3-hir.json. All tests live in axiom-hir crate as unit tests (#[cfg(test)] modules).

test_functions.name: test_hir_coverage
test_functions.description: Verifies that all AST construct types can be lowered to HIR
test_functions.approach: Build an AST module containing every variant (function, struct, type alias, import, let, assign, return, if/else, for, while, expr statement, binary op, unary op, call, index, field access, method call, int/float/string/bool literals, ident, opt hole) and lower it. Assert no errors and all HIR node types are present.
assertions: HirModule has functions, structs, type_aliases, imports
Function body contains Let, Assign, Return, If, For, While, Expr stmts

test_functions.name: test_lower_fibonacci
test_functions.description: Parse fibonacci.axm source, lower to HIR, verify structure
test_functions.approach: Use axiom_parser::parse on the fibonacci source, then lower. Assert no errors. Check that the module has 2 functions (fib, main), fib has @pure and @complexity annotations, fib takes one i32 param and returns i64.

test_functions.name: test_annotation_preservation
test_functions.description: Verify that semantic annotations survive lowering intact
test_functions.approach: Build AST with @pure, @intent, @complexity, @inline, @constraint, @strategy on a function. Lower. Assert all annotations are present in the HIR function's annotations vec with correct data.
assertions: HirAnnotationKind::Intent with correct string is present
HirAnnotationKind::Complexity with correct expr is present
HirAnnotationKind::Inline with correct hint is present

test_functions.name: test_annotation_validation
test_functions.description: Verify that invalid annotation targets are rejected

test_cases.input: @pure on a let binding (via block annotation on non-function)
test_cases.expected_error: InvalidAnnotationTarget { annotation: "pure", target: contains "param" or similar }

test_functions.name: test_annotation_validation_valid_targets
test_functions.description: Verify that valid annotation targets are accepted

test_functions.name: test_type_validation
test_functions.description: Verify type validation catches unknown types and resolves known ones

test_cases.input: param of type MyStruct (where MyStruct is defined as a struct)

test_functions.name: test_type_validation_forward_reference
test_functions.description: A function using a struct type that is defined later in the file should resolve correctly
assertions: No errors when struct is defined after function that uses it

test_functions.name: test_display_hello
test_functions.description: Pretty-print hello.axm HIR, verify output contains 'fn main'

test_suites.implementation: Run cargo clippy -p axiom-hir -- -D warnings. Ensure code follows Rust idioms: no unnecessary clones, proper use of &self vs self, etc.

edge_cases.input: No items, no annotations, no module name
edge_cases.expected: Valid HirModule with empty vecs and name=None

edge_cases.case: Module with only annotations, no items
edge_cases.expected: Valid HirModule with annotations but empty functions/structs
LowerError::DuplicateDefinition emitted, both functions still lowered

edge_cases.case: Self-referential struct (struct containing its own type)
edge_cases.expected: Accepted at HIR level — type validation only checks existence, not layout feasibility. Phase 2 can reject recursive types without indirection.
LowerError::UnknownType for 'Unknown', alias lowered with HirType::Unknown

edge_cases.input: a + b * c - d / e + f
edge_cases.expected: Correctly lowered nested BinaryOp tree. No stack overflow for reasonable depths.
Valid HirFunction with empty body block. (Missing return is a semantic error for Phase 2, not HIR lowering.)

edge_cases.input: Import declarations don't carry annotations in the AST
edge_cases.expected: Lowered without annotations, no validation issue

edge_cases.input: Function using every primitive type as params
edge_cases.expected: All 15 primitive types resolve to correct PrimitiveType variants
Lowered as HirExprKind::OptHole("hole_name"), no validation error at HIR level
Nested MethodCall HIR nodes, correctly structured

implementation_order.description: Define all HIR node types, PrimitiveType, NodeId, SPAN_DUMMY. This is the foundation everything else depends on.
Define LowerError and AnnotationTarget enums with thiserror/miette derives.
Add dependencies on axiom-lexer, axiom-parser, miette.
Implement LoweringContext and the full lowering pass with annotation validation and type validation. Include all unit tests.
Implement Display for HirModule and all HIR types. Include display tests.
Replace stub with module declarations and re-exports.
Wire up --emit=hir arm in the match statement.

validation_checklist: cargo build -p axiom-hir compiles without errors
cargo test -p axiom-hir passes all tests
cargo clippy -p axiom-hir -- -D warnings produces no warnings
cargo run -p axiom-driver -- compile --emit=hir tests/samples/fibonacci.axm prints HIR containing 'fn fib'
cargo run -p axiom-driver -- compile --emit=hir tests/samples/hello.axm prints HIR containing 'fn main'
All acceptance criteria from M1.3-hir.json are satisfied
